evidence: Accuracy strongly resonates with ontological.informational (10) as it directly relates to factual correctness. Resonance.essential (10) reflects its core role in truth. Functional value (10) underscores its practical utility. Lower scores in catalytic and chained dynamics show how accuracy influences systems and change. Scores in symmetry.transformative/generative (3) and lawfulness.contingent (2) highlight its role in enabling reliable outcomes.

Dependability strongly resonates with semantic meaning (5) as it embodies truth's value, functional utility (5) for reliability, and relational trust (4). It also aligns with predictability (4) and comprehension (2) of consistent truth.

Dependable strongly resonates with semantic (core meaning of truth), predictable (reliability implies consistency), functional (practical utility of trust), and ethical (moral dimension of reliability). Lower scores reflect its focus on relational stability and comprehension of truth's foundations.

Dependableness as truth resonates most with relational/semantic layers (trust between entities, core meaning), essential resonance (touching truth's essence), ethical value (moral reliability), and predictable lawfulness (reliability over time). It operates at individual scales and through catalytic interconnections (triggering trust), while avoiding destructive symmetry (falsehood's pathology).

Dishonesty resonates most with truth's inverse. It directly opposes truth's essence (resonance.essential), operates at individual/community levels (scale.individual/community), and corrupts relational trust (ontological.relational). Its destructive nature (symmetry.destructive) and ethical implications (value_generation.ethical) are core. It catalyzes effects (interconnection.catalytic) and depends on context (lawfulness.contingent).

Downright emphasizes the core essence (resonance.essential) and moral value (value_generation.ethical) of truth, intensifying its semantic meaning (ontological.semantic) and catalyzing comprehension (experience.comprehension).

Exactness strongly resonates with ontological.semantic (10) as it defines the core meaning of truth. High scores in epistemological.understanding (8) and lawfulness.predictable (9) reflect precision in grasping truth and adherence to patterns. Scores in structural and functional slots (7-8) highlight exactness's role in precise systems and practical value.

Exactitude resonates strongly with semantic meaning (ontological.semantic), informational precision (ontological.informational), and ethical value (value_generation.ethical). It emphasizes precise understanding (epistemological.understanding) and structural truth (ontological.structural). Its necessity (lawfulness.necessary) and role in societal systems (scale.society) further highlight its core essence (resonance.essential).

Exactness resonates most with semantic meaning (9), essential truth (7), and existential depth (5). It operates at informational (8) and relational (6) layers, underpins deep understanding (8), and aligns with functional (6) and ethical (5) value. Predictability (7) reflects its role in stable truth.

Falsity most strongly resonates with slots tied to its opposition to truth (ontological.semantic, value_generation.ethical), its presence in social systems (scale.society, scale.individual), and its destructive impact (symmetry.destructive). Lower scores reflect its transient, non-permanent nature and lack of cosmic/ecological relevance.

Fidelity in truth strongly resonates with semantic meaning (ontological.semantic), ethical value (value_generation.ethical), and core essence (resonance.essential). It connects to individual experience (epistemological.experience) and predictable patterns (lawfulness.predictable). Weaker ties exist to informational layers (ontological.informational), resonant interconnections (interconnection.resonant), and comprehension (experience.comprehension).

Geographic resonates most with spatial and material truths (ontological.material, ontological.informational), factual consistency (lawfulness.predictable), and societal-scale boundaries (scale.society). Scores reflect its grounding in physical reality and structured knowledge, with weaker ties to relational patterns and transformative dynamics.

Geographical truth resonates most with material existence (physical landscapes), societal scales (national boundaries), and epistemological processes like identification (naming regions) and structural understanding (maps). Its functional utility in practical contexts (e.g., navigation) and perceptual grounding in tangible environments drive higher scores. Lower scores reflect its lack of direct ties to existential or ethical value.

Honesty strongly resonates with ontological.relational (trust between people), resonance.essential (core of truth), and value_generation.ethical (moral imperative). Moderate scores reflect its role in epistemological.understanding/experience (embodied truth), interconnection.resonant (deep mutual trust), and symmetry.transformative (catalyzing trust).

Honestness resonates strongly with individual and relational layers (personal integrity and trust between people), ethical value (right/wrong), and deep mutual resonance (trust-based relationships). It operates predictably in stable contexts and catalyzes trust over time.

Honorable strongly resonates with ethical value (7), semantic meaning (7), and the essential core of truth's value (3). It connects to individual/community/society scales, relational/social structures, and comprehension/experience of truth's moral significance.

Honorableness strongly resonates with ethical value (10), relational ontology (7), and semantic meaning (8). It operates at individual/community scales (8/5) and influences trust over time (temporal.influence=4, continuation=6). Its essential resonance (7) ties to core truthfulness, contrasting with the symmetric pair VAL.falsehood.

Inaccuracy strongly resonates with opposition to truth (symmetry.destructive 5), semantic layer (ontological.semantic 5), and ethical implications (value_generation.ethical 5). It operates at individual/community scales (scale.individual 5, scale.community 4), influences over time (temporal.influence 4), and has structural patterns (resonance.structural 4).

Minute resonates with slots tied to small but impactful details in truth. High scores reflect its role in catalyzing understanding (interconnection.catalytic), revealing structural patterns (ontological.semantic), and enabling comprehension through subtle clues (experience.comprehension). Scores for functional value and superficial resonance highlight its practical utility and surface-level presence in factual verification.

Precision in truth resonates most with epistemological understanding and creation (grasping structure, generating accurate knowledge), ontological informational/semantic layers (data accuracy and meaning), and lawfulness predictability (alignment with established patterns). Lower scores reflect precision's focus on factual alignment rather than existential or relational dynamics.

Reliability in truth strongly resonates with temporal.continuation (ongoing trustworthiness), ontological.semantic (core value of truth), and lawfulness.predictable (consistent adherence to fact). Moderate scores reflect its relational nature (ontological.relational), societal utility (value_generation.functional), and ethical dimension (value_generation.ethical).

Reliable resonates most with temporal.continuation (3), scale.individual (5), epistemological.experience (5), ontological.semantic (5), resonance.essential (5), lawfulness.predictable (5), and value.functional (5). These reflect its core role in consistent adherence to truth over time, personal trustworthiness, experiential grounding, semantic value, and practical utility. Lower scores reflect its lack of connection to transformational dynamics or cosmic scales.

Reliableness as truth strongly resonates with its essential nature (resonance.essential) and semantic meaning (ontological.semantic). It operates at individual/community/society scales (scale.individual, scale.community, scale.society) and relies on understanding (epistemological.understanding). It has ethical value (value_generation.ethical) and is predictably foundational (lawfulness.predictable).

Sojourner Truth's advocacy for moral and social justice deeply aligns with VAL.truth through her lived experience (epistemological.experience 8) and structural impact on societal values (ontological.structural 8). Her speeches and activism catalyzed transformative change (symmetry.transformative 8) and resonate existentially as ethical (9) and sacred (8) truths, persisting across time (temporal.continuation 8).

The true strongly resonates with permanence (timeless quality), semantic ontology (core meaning), and essential/existential resonance (touching fundamental truth). It moderately connects with individual/society scales (personal and societal truths), understanding/experience (grasping and living truth), and ethical/sacred value (moral and transcendent dimensions). Scores are low elsewhere as the atom focuses purely on the abstract quality of truth itself.

The word 'true' strongly resonates with semantic meaning (5), ethical implications (4), and existential/necessary lawfulness (4). It also connects to permanence (3), comprehension (3), and foundational structures (ontological.semantic, resonance.essential). Scores reflect its core role in truth's essence and value systems.

Trueness resonates most with semantic meaning (10), foundational understanding (9), and ethical value (8). It operates at individual/community scales (5/3) and through catalytic/resonant interconnections (5/6). Scores drop to 0 where trueness lacks direct ties to physicality, cosmic scale, or destructive dynamics.

Trustworthiness strongly resonates with individual scale (10), relational ontology (7), and ethical value (8). It connects to lived experience (6), understanding (5), and predictable behavior (6). Lower scores reflect its core alignment with truth's essence and relational dynamics, avoiding overreach into cosmic or destructive contexts.

The word 'trusty' strongly resonates with semantic slots emphasizing reliability over time (temporal.continuation, permanence), individual trustworthiness (scale.individual), and semantic meaning (ontological.semantic). It also aligns with lawful predictability and ethical value, contrasting with the symmetric pair VAL.falsehood by avoiding destructive or contingent scores.

Truth resonates most strongly with semantic.essential (10), reflecting its core essence. It aligns with ontological.informational (7) as a data layer and epistemological.understanding (7) for grasping structures. Scale slots (individual, community, society) reflect its applicability across social layers. Ethical (7) and necessary (6) highlight its moral and inevitable aspects. Most slots score 0 due to strict adherence to the atom's definition and the requirement for minimal over-scoring.

Verity strongly resonates with the semantic layer (core meaning of truth), essential resonance (touching the essence of existence), ethical value (moral dimension), and the necessity of truths. It also connects to understanding, transformative dynamics, and the practicality of truth in guiding comprehension and action.
